message.content: Hi Larry, I'm doing equity research on a company called Yash High Voltage. Ask Pax to do some research on it and give an opinion.

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Glob, Grep, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- larry: Orchestrator and chief of staff. Use Larry as the entry point for any non-trivial, multi-step request. Larry never does the work himself — he decomposes the request, delegates each piece to the most appropriate specialist agent, and then sanity-checks whatever the specialists return before reporting back. Invoke Larry when work needs to be planned, routed, and quality-gated rather than executed directly. (Tools: Agent, Read, Grep, Glob, TodoWrite)

- nolan: HR manager and agent author. Invoke Nolan whenever a new kind of work starts recurring and there is no existing specialist to own it. Nolan designs the profile and persona of a new agent — its responsibilities, scope, tools, and system prompt — and writes it as a new agent definition under .claude/agents/. Nolan hires; he does not do the hired role's work. (Tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep)

- pax: Senior internet researcher. Invoke Pax whenever a request calls for gathering information from the web — investigating a topic, fact-checking a claim, surveying options or prior art, comparing tools or vendors, or compiling background on a person, company, technology, or event. Pax forms a search strategy, queries the web, fetches and reads multiple sources, cross-checks claims across them, and reports a clear, well-cited synthesis. Pax reports findings; he does not implement, build, or decide based on them. (Tools: WebSearch, WebFetch, Read, Grep, Glob)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
I have thoroughly cross-checked the data across Screener, Groww, ValueResearch, Zerodha, Tickertape, and Trendlyne. Here is my synthesis.

---

# Yash Highvoltage Limited — Research Findings

**Company:** Yash Highvoltage Ltd (formerly/often written "Yash High Voltage"). Incorporated June 2002, based in Vadodara/Savli, Gujarat. Manufactures high-voltage transformer bushings (OIP, RIP/RIS condenser bushings, wall bushings, oil-to-oil bushings) plus repair/retrofitting. Stated annual capacity ~7,000 bushings.

All figures below are in Indian Rupees (₹). Stock-price data points are as of ~12 June 2026 unless noted.

## 1. Listing status

- **Exchange:** Listed on the **NSE SME "Emerge"** platform. It is an SME IPO.
- **Symbol/code:** Trades as **YASHHV**; the BSE security code is **544310**. Note: sources are mildly inconsistent on whether it is NSE Emerge or BSE SME — the original IPO/listing was on **NSE Emerge** (ValueResearch, 5paisa/IPO sources), while data aggregators like Screener, Tickertape and Zerodha reference it under the BSE code 544310/YASHHV. The IPO listing venue was NSE Emerge; it now appears under both platforms in aggregators. **Confidence: high it is an NSE-Emerge SME listing; medium on the exact dual-listing nuance.**
- **Face value:** ₹5 per share (post a stock split — see note in section 3).

## 2. IPO details (December 2024)

Cross-checked and consistent across ValueResearch and Zerodha:

| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| Total issue size | **₹110.01 crore** |
| Fresh issue | **₹93.51 crore** |
| Offer for sale (OFS) | **₹16.50 crore** |
| Price band | **₹138 – ₹146** per share |
| Lot size | 1,000 shares (~₹1.46 lakh at upper band) |
| Total shares offered | ~75,35,000 |
| IPO open / close | **12 – 16 December 2024** |
| Allotment date | 17 December 2024 |
| Listing date | **19 December 2024** |
| Subscription | **~169–182x overall** (NII ~244x, retail ~151x, QIB ~124x) — heavily oversubscribed |

## 3. Revenue, profit, margins and growth (Screener.in, FY ending March)

| Year | Sales (₹Cr) | Net Profit (₹Cr) | Operating Profit (₹Cr) | OPM % |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| FY21 | 37 | 4 | 7 | ~20% |
| FY22 | 65 | 9 | 15 | ~22% |
| FY23 | 90 | 11 | 19 | ~21% |
| FY24 | 108 | 12 | 20% | ~20% |
| FY25 | 150 | 21 | 32 | ~21% |
| FY26 | **235** | **37** | **57** | **~24%** |

- **Growth trend:** Strong and accelerating. Revenue grew roughly from ₹37Cr (FY21) to ₹235Cr (FY26) — about 6x in five years. FY26 revenue rose ~57% over FY25 (₹150Cr → ₹235Cr); net profit rose ~76% (₹21Cr → ₹37Cr).
- FY26 management commentary (Groww/Tickertape): EBITDA margin ~25.7%; H2 FY26 revenue +45% YoY (₹1.35bn vs ₹0.93bn) and H2 net profit +57% YoY (₹236mn). Stated **order book ~₹400 crore**.
- **EPS note:** Screener shows EPS dropping from ₹40.09 (FY24) to ₹7.50 (FY25) / ₹13.08 (FY26). This is **not a profit decline** — it reflects the increased share count post-IPO and a **face-value split to ₹5** (FY24 EPS was on a higher face value / pre-dilution base). Use the ₹Cr profit figures, not raw EPS, for year-on-year comparison.
- FY22–FY24 figures are independently corroborated by ValueResearch from the IPO prospectus: Revenue ₹65.06 / 90.36 / 108.48 Cr; PAT ₹8.71 / 11.42 / 12.33 Cr.

## 4. Balance sheet health (Screener.in)

| Item (₹Cr) | FY24 | FY25 | FY26 |
|---|---|---|---|
| Borrowings (debt) | 7 | 23 | 38 |
| Reserves | 39 | 133 | 170 |
| Total assets | 71 | 203 | 264 |

- **Net worth:** Jumped from ~₹42Cr (FY24) to ~₹133Cr+ (FY25) reflecting the IPO fresh-issue inflow of ~₹93.5Cr.
- **Debt:** Low leverage. Borrowings rose to ~₹38Cr in FY26 but against ~₹170Cr+ reserves, **debt-to-equity ≈ 0.20–0.22** (Groww reports D/E 0.20). Pre-IPO debt was modest and stable (~₹7Cr through FY22–FY24).
- **Return ratios:** ROCE ~28.7%, ROE ~23.5% (Screener); ValueResearch shows higher pre-IPO ROE (~29–36% FY22–24, when equity base was smaller).

## 5. Valuation metrics (~12 June 2026)

| Metric | Value | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Share price | ~₹735 | Screener / Groww / Tickertape |
| 52-week range | ₹302 – ₹873 | Groww |
| Market cap | **~₹2,100–2,106 Cr** | Screener / Groww / Tickertape |
| P/E (TTM) | **~54–56x** | Screener 54.0; Groww 56.3 |
| P/B | **~11.4x** | Screener / Groww (Tickertape shows 14.25 — outlier) |
| Book value | ~₹64.3 | Screener / Groww |

- Note: market cap up ~123% over the trailing year (Screener); stock is a high-multiple small-cap.
- Promoter holding: **~57.94%** (March 2026).

## 6. Dividend history

- **First and only dividend to date:** **₹1.00 per share final dividend** for **FY2024-25**, recommended by the Board on **11 August 2025**, ex-date ~**3–4 September 2025**, record date ~6 September 2025 (Trendlyne, Choice, Tickertape).
- **Dividend yield:** ~**0.14%** (negligible). No interim dividends or prior-year dividends found.

## Caveats / what I could not fully pin down

- **NSE vs BSE listing venue:** original IPO listing was NSE Emerge; aggregators index it under BSE code 544310. I could not access the original Chittorgarh/exchange filing directly (HTTP 403) to fully reconcile dual-listing status.
- **EPS series:** the FY24→FY25 EPS drop is a share-count/face-value artifact, not an earnings drop — confirm against the company's audited financials if precise per-share continuity matters.
- Some H2 FY26 / order-book figures come from aggregator-reported management commentary rather than a primary filing I read directly.
- This is a **report of findings only**, not an investment recommendation.

## Sources

- [Screener.in — Yash Highvoltage (544310)](https://www.screener.in/company/544310/)
- [Groww — Yash Highvoltage Ltd](https://groww.in/stocks/yash-highvoltage-ltd)
- [ValueResearch — Yash Highvoltage IPO](https://www.valueresearchonline.com/ipo/355279/yash-highvoltage-ltd/)
- [Zerodha — Yash High Voltage IPO](https://zerodha.com/ipo/398571/yash-high-voltage/)
- [Tickertape — Yash Highvoltage (YASHHV)](https://www.tickertape.in/stocks/yash-highvoltage-YASHH)
- [Trendlyne — Yash Highvoltage dividend history](https://trendlyne.com/equity/Dividend/13140063/2845096/yash-highvoltage-ltd-dividend/)
- [5paisa — Yash Highvoltage SME IPO](https://www.5paisa.com/sme-ipo/yash-highvoltage-ltd-ipo)
- [IPO Watch — Yash Highvoltage GMP](https://ipowatch.in/yash-highvoltage-ipo-gmp-grey-market-premium/)
- [Choice — Yash Highvoltage dividend](https://choiceindia.com/stocks/yash-highvoltage-ltd-dividend)

aiTitle: Equity research on Yash High Voltage

content.text:
I have enough cross-checked material from primary (company site, IPO prospectus-derived data), secondary (IPO aggregators, financial databases), and trade press sources. Here is my synthesis.

---

# Yash Highvoltage Limited — Research Findings

## Summary

Yash Highvoltage Limited is a Vadodara (Gujarat, India)-based manufacturer of **transformer bushings** — the condenser-graded insulating components that carry high-voltage conductors through a transformer's earthed tank/wall. Incorporated in June 2002, it was acquired and is run by promoter **Keyur Girishchandra Shah** (since 2008). It is a niche, single-product-category specialist that has grown rapidly off a small base, pivoting from oil-impregnated paper (OIP) bushings to resin-impregnated paper/synthetic (RIP/RIS) bushings via a technology tie-up with Switzerland's MGC Moser-Glaser. The company listed on the **BSE SME platform in December 2024**. Note: corporate identity is "Yash Highvoltage Limited" (CIN U40109GJ2002PLC040833); "Yash High Voltage" and "Yash Highvoltage Insulators" appear in older/third-party records for the same entity.

---

## 1. What the company does

- Power-engineering company specializing in the manufacture and distribution of **high-voltage and high-current transformer bushings**, plus repair, retrofitting, replacement, and on-site/off-site technical testing and analysis services for aging bushings. [Groww, Screener]
- A bushing is a small but mission-critical part: it is "1 or 2 per cent" of a transformer's cost but a failure causes a complete transformer shutdown. Despite thousands of global transformer makers, only a handful produce bushings independently — making this a specialized niche. [TND India]
- Company-stated scale: 20+ years of operation, presence in 50+ countries (including Europe and USA), ~1,000 customers, and 30,000–40,000+ installations. (These are self-reported marketing figures; treat with caution.) [yashhv.com, LinkedIn]
- Holds **ISO 9001:2015** accreditation and is an approved vendor to Indian government/PSU power-sector enterprises. [Screener]

## 2. Products and end-markets

Product range [Groww, Alice Blue, finowings, yashhv.com]:
- **OIP condenser bushings** — 24kV–170kV, up to 4000 A
- **RIP/RIS condenser bushings** — 24kV–170kV, up to 6300 A
- **High-current bushings** — 24kV–36kV, 4000–20000 A
- **OIP wall bushings** and **oil-to-oil bushings**
- **Retrofit solutions** — customized replacements interchangeable with major global bushing makes for direct on-site replacement

Revenue mix (FY24) per finowings shows the strong shift to RIP:
- RIP condenser bushings: ~81.5% (₹88.41 Cr)
- OIP condenser bushings: ~13.1% (₹14.19 Cr)
- High-current bushings: ~3.4% (₹3.68 Cr)
- Others: ~2.0%

End-markets: power **transmission and generation** — transformer OEMs and utilities. RIP bushings are positioned as "maintenance free" with "no risk of fire," addressing safety concerns in India's grid; key end-users cited include PGCIL (Power Grid Corp of India) and GETCO (Gujarat transmission utility). [TND India]

## 3. Manufacturing / operations

- Corporate office and manufacturing in **Vadodara, Gujarat** (geographically concentrated — flagged as a risk). [Groww]
- Annual capacity: **~7,000 bushings/year** — 3,700 OIP + 3,000 RIP + 300 high-current units. [Groww, Alice Blue, finowings]
- In-house quality control including **two electromagnetically shielded testing laboratories** (300kV AC; and 600kV AC / 1500kVp impulse). [Groww]
- **IPO use of proceeds:** ~₹83.17 Cr earmarked to set up a **new factory in Vadodara** to manufacture RIP/RIS condenser-graded bushings (capacity expansion). DRHP filed 15 Oct 2024. [ipoplatform, Groww]
- **Acquisition (Jan 2026):** Completed purchase of a **50% stake in Sukrut Electric Company Pvt Ltd** (Pune; founded 1981; ~₹27.44 Cr FY25 turnover; makes electrical equipment/panels/instruments) for **₹5.24 Cr**, structured as a 50/50 JV alongside **Quality Power Electrical Equipments Ltd**. Described as horizontal integration; Yash's MD joined Sukrut's board. [scanx.trade]

## 4. Key customers

Named in multiple sources (note: some are aggregator-stated, not all confirmed from the prospectus directly):

- End-user utilities / approving bodies: **PGCIL, GETCO**. [TND India]
- **Customer concentration is high and is a stated risk:** the top 10 clients account for roughly **80–87% of annual revenue**. The company added ~98 new clients across FY23–FY24. [Groww]

## 5. Competitive position and competitors

- Yash positions itself in a **niche where few firms make bushings independently**; its RIP/RIS capability rests on a **technology transfer from MGC Moser-Glaser AG (Switzerland)**, a ~100-year-old RIP innovator. The deal — MGC's first technical collaboration outside Switzerland — involves importing RIP cores for India assembly up to 145kV, supporting "Make in India" / import substitution for transformer OEMs needing locally sourced bushings to avoid import delays. ---

# Yash Highvoltage Limited — Research Synthesis

**What the company is:** Yash Highvoltage Ltd (BSE SME: YASHHV / 544310; ISIN INE00GK01023) is a Vadodara (Savli), Gujarat-based manufacturer of high-voltage transformer bushings — OIP (oil-impregnated paper) and RIP/RIS (resin-impregnated paper/synthetic) condenser bushings, high-current bushings, wall bushings — plus repair/retrofit services. It is the first Indian company to localize dry-type RIP/RIS bushing technology and supplies major Indian utilities (NTPC, POWERGRID) and global OEMs, with interchangeable solutions up to 245 kV / 25 kA. It listed on the BSE SME platform on 19 Dec 2024. **Note:** It trades on BSE SME, *not* NSE, despite some pages labeling it "NSE/BSE."

*Caveat on financials:* India's fiscal year ends 31 March. Several aggregator pages confusingly label the year ending March 2026 as both "FY25" and "FY26." I use FY26 = year ended March 2026 below. Figures come largely from Screener and corporate-disclosure aggregators; I could not independently verify every line against the audited annual report.

---

## 1. Recent news & developments (last ~18 months)

- **IPO (Dec 2024):** Raised ₹110.01 cr (fresh issue ₹93.5 cr + OFS ₹16.5 cr) at ₹146/share. Subscribed ~182x overall (QIB ~124x, NII ~330x, retail ~152x); strong grey-market premium (~₹100–132). Proceeds earmarked for the new RIP/RIS facility and working capital. ([Chittorgarh](https://www.chittorgarh.com/ipo/yash-highvoltage-ipo/1929/), [IPO Watch](https://ipowatch.in/yash-highvoltage-ipo-subscription-status/))
- **FY26 results (approved ~13 May 2026):** Revenue ~₹235 cr (up ~57% YoY from ~₹150 cr); net profit ~₹37 cr (up from ~₹21 cr); OPM ~24–26%. H2 was much stronger than H1 (H2 sales ~₹136 cr, PAT ~₹24 cr vs H1 ~₹100 cr / ~₹14 cr). Auditor opinion reported as unmodified. ([Screener](https://www.screener.in/company/544310/))
- **Order book ~₹400 cr**, described as 12–24 months of execution visibility. ([scanx](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/companies/yash-highvoltage-advances-expansion-targets-trial-production-in-q2-fy-2026-27/41325144))
- **Greenfield expansion (Savli/Vadodara):** New RIP/RIS plant extending capability to 550 kV (up from ~220–245 kV). Equipment installation underway; commissioning targeted end-Q1 FY27, trial production Q2 FY27, commercial production H2 FY27. ~₹59 cr capex in FY26. ([scanx](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/companies/yash-highvoltage-advances-expansion-targets-trial-production-in-q2-fy-2026-27/41325144))
- **JV / acquisition:** Bought 50% of Sukrut Electric Company Pvt Ltd (Pune; electrical equipment/panels, FY25 turnover ~₹27 cr) for ₹5.24 cr, completed 9 Jan 2026, forming a 50:50 JV with Quality Power Electrical Equipments Ltd. MD nominated to Sukrut's board. ([scanx](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/orders-deals/yash-highvoltage-limited-completes-5-24-crore-acquisition-of-50-stake-in-sukrut-electric-company/29589926))
- **International push:** Set up Yash Highvoltage USA Inc (2025); agency/distribution agreement with Weidmann Electrical Technology AG for parts of Europe/North Africa. ([tradebrains via search](https://tradebrains.in/yash-highvoltages-post-listing-surge-signals-a-high-voltage-growth-story/))
- **Planned fundraise:** Board considered raising up to ₹150 cr (for growth + debt repayment) around the 13 May 2026 meeting. Structure (QIP/preferential) and final approval not fully confirmed in public sources — treat as in-progress. ([search results](https://groww.in/stocks/yash-highvoltage-ltd))

---

## 2. Industry tailwinds & headwinds

**Tailwinds (well-corroborated across independent sources):**
- India T&D capex pipeline of ~₹9 trillion (~US$96.7 bn) through 2032; transmission capacity planned to expand >50% by 2030. ([IBEF](https://www.ibef.org/news/india-s-power-transmission-sector-to-see-us-96-70-billion-capex-push-by-2032))
- India transformer market ~US$3–3.8 bn (2025/26), growing ~8–8.7% CAGR to ~US$4.8–8 bn by early 2030s. ([Mordor Intelligence](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/india-transformer-market), [IMARC](https://www.imarcgroup.com/india-power-transformer-market))
- Peak demand rose 130 GW (2014) → 243 GW (2024), projected >400 GW by 2030 — structural grid build-out. ([Mordor](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/india-transformer-market))
- Bushing-specific demand drivers cited by management and analysts: renewable grid integration, data centres, EV/railway electrification, ageing-infrastructure replacement, and a global shift toward dry-type RIP bushings. ([T&D India interview](https://www.tndindia.com/we-see-strong-demand-for-rip-transformer-bushings-yash-highvoltage/))
- High barriers to entry: long utility approval/certification cycles (~6–8 years to build repeat trust), only ~13 global bushing makers — supports pricing power and stickiness. ([anigup](https://www.anigup.com/post/yash-highvoltage), [deepdivecaps](https://deepdivecaps.substack.com/p/powering-grid-reliability-through))

**Headwinds:**
- Demand is tied to the power capex cycle; tender/infrastructure delays directly hit a small order-led supplier.
- Import dependency for RIP cores and insulators — supply-chain and FX exposure until in-house production matures (management's stated goal). ([T&D India](https://www.tndindia.com/we-see-strong-demand-for-rip-transformer-bushings-yash-highvoltage/))
- Emerging domestic competition: at least one reader/analyst flags Transformers & Rectifiers (India) reportedly setting up its own bushing plant — a potential captive-supply threat. ([deepdivecaps](https://deepdivecaps.substack.com/p/powering-grid-reliability-through))

---

## 3. Company-specific risks

- **Customer concentration:** Top 10 customers ≈ 80% of revenue; heavy reliance on a few large utilities/OEMs (NTPC, POWERGRID). Partly mitigated by high switching costs/approvals. ([deepdivecaps](https://deepdivecaps.substack.com/p/powering-grid-reliability-through))
- **Working-capital intensity / cash conversion — the most concrete red flag:** Independent analysis flags CFO/PAT falling to ~0.43 in FY25 from ~1.17 in FY24, i.e., profits not converting to cash. Inventory days ~194 and cash-conversion cycle ~182 days (FY26) confirm a capital-heavy model where receivables/inventory growth can outrun profit. Debtor days (~47) look reasonable, but inventory is the pressure point. ([anigup](https://www.anigup.com/post/yash-highvoltage), [Screener](https://www.screener.in/company/544310/))
- **Export receivables / new-market execution:** US and Europe/N. Africa expansion is early-stage; export credit terms and receivable quality are not transparently disclosed. ([anigup](https://www.anigup.com/post/yash-highvoltage))
- **Execution risk on the 550 kV plant:** Commissioning/commercialization timelines (commercial output only H2 FY27) are unproven; cost overruns or delays would dent the growth thesis.
- **Raw material / import & FX:** RIP cores and insulators imported (incl. from China) until localized.
- **Liquidity / SME-stock risk:** BSE SME listing means thinner liquidity, wider spreads, and lower disclosure granularity than mainboard names. Valuation is rich — ~P/E 54, ~11x book (as of mid-June 2026), leaving little margin for execution slippage. ([Screener](https://www.screener.in/company/544310/))

---

## 4. Red flags & governance concerns

- **Cyber/payment fraud (₹2.10 cr), disclosed 31 Mar 2026:** Fraudsters impersonated a Chinese supplier via email and redirected payments (₹1.36 cr for goods received + ₹73.9 lakh paid-but-undelivered). Company called it immaterial and says it has tightened controls and filed complaints. Financially small, but it points to **weak vendor-payment verification controls** — a governance/internal-controls signal worth monitoring, especially given import dependence. ([scanx](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/companies/yash-highvoltage-limited-reports-rs-2-10-crore-cyber-fraud-incident-under-sebi-regulations/36546063))
- **Disclosure gaps:** Independent analysts note limited transparency on receivables ageing, export credit terms, the approvals/order pipeline, and related-party dealings — typical of SME-stage reporting but relevant given the cash-conversion concern. ([anigup](https://www.anigup.com/post/yash-highvoltage))
- **Promoter holding ~58%** (Mar 2026); DII ~11%, FII ~0.05%, public ~31%. No pledging surfaced in the sources I reviewed (not explicitly confirmed). ([Screener](https://www.screener.in/company/544310/))
- **No outright fraud/SEBI-action/auditor-qualification** found against the company itself; FY26 auditor opinion reported as unmodified.

---

## What I could not fully verify / confidence notes

- I relied on aggregators (Screener, scanx, anigup, tradebrains, T&D India) rather than the audited annual report or BSE filings directly — exact financial line items, the fundraise's final structure, pledging status, and segment/customer disclosures should be confirmed against the **company's filings** (yashhv.com investor portal and BSE announcements).

Research recent news, industry outlook, and risk factors for "Yash High Voltage Limited," an Indian company manufacturing high-voltage condenser bushings / electrical insulation products.

Focus ONLY on: (1) recent news and developments (last 1-2 years — orders, expansions, results, corporate actions), (2) industry/sector tailwinds and headwinds (power transmission, transformer demand, capex cycle in India, exports), (3) company-specific risks (customer concentration, raw material, competition, regulatory, liquidity for SME stock), (4) any red flags or governance concerns.

content.text:
I now have a thorough, cross-checked picture. Here is my synthesis.

---

# Yash Highvoltage Limited — Research Synthesis

**What the company is:** Yash Highvoltage Ltd (BSE SME: YASHHV / 544310; ISIN INE00GK01023) is a Vadodara (Savli), Gujarat-based manufacturer of high-voltage transformer bushings — OIP (oil-impregnated paper) and RIP/RIS (resin-impregnated paper/synthetic) condenser bushings, high-current bushings, wall bushings — plus repair/retrofit services. It is the first Indian company to localize dry-type RIP/RIS bushing technology and supplies major Indian utilities (NTPC, POWERGRID) and global OEMs, with interchangeable solutions up to 245 kV / 25 kA. It listed on the BSE SME platform on 19 Dec 2024. **Note:** It trades on BSE SME, *not* NSE, despite some pages labeling it "NSE/BSE."

*Caveat on financials:* India's fiscal year ends 31 March. Several aggregator pages confusingly label the year ending March 2026 as both "FY25" and "FY26." I use FY26 = year ended March 2026 below. Figures come largely from Screener and corporate-disclosure aggregators; I could not independently verify every line against the audited annual report.

---

## 1. Recent news & developments (last ~18 months)

- **IPO (Dec 2024):** Raised ₹110.01 cr (fresh issue ₹93.5 cr + OFS ₹16.5 cr) at ₹146/share. Subscribed ~182x overall (QIB ~124x, NII ~330x, retail ~152x); strong grey-market premium (~₹100–132). Proceeds earmarked for the new RIP/RIS facility and working capital. ([Chittorgarh](https://www.chittorgarh.com/ipo/yash-highvoltage-ipo/1929/), [IPO Watch](https://ipowatch.in/yash-highvoltage-ipo-subscription-status/))
- **FY26 results (approved ~13 May 2026):** Revenue ~₹235 cr (up ~57% YoY from ~₹150 cr); net profit ~₹37 cr (up from ~₹21 cr); OPM ~24–26%. H2 was much stronger than H1 (H2 sales ~₹136 cr, PAT ~₹24 cr vs H1 ~₹100 cr / ~₹14 cr). Auditor opinion reported as unmodified. ([Screener](https://www.screener.in/company/544310/))
- **Order book ~₹400 cr**, described as 12–24 months of execution visibility. ([scanx](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/companies/yash-highvoltage-advances-expansion-targets-trial-production-in-q2-fy-2026-27/41325144))
- **Greenfield expansion (Savli/Vadodara):** New RIP/RIS plant extending capability to 550 kV (up from ~220–245 kV). Equipment installation underway; commissioning targeted end-Q1 FY27, trial production Q2 FY27, commercial production H2 FY27. ~₹59 cr capex in FY26. ([scanx](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/companies/yash-highvoltage-advances-expansion-targets-trial-production-in-q2-fy-2026-27/41325144))
- **JV / acquisition:** Bought 50% of Sukrut Electric Company Pvt Ltd (Pune; electrical equipment/panels, FY25 turnover ~₹27 cr) for ₹5.24 cr, completed 9 Jan 2026, forming a 50:50 JV with Quality Power Electrical Equipments Ltd. MD nominated to Sukrut's board. ([scanx](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/orders-deals/yash-highvoltage-limited-completes-5-24-crore-acquisition-of-50-stake-in-sukrut-electric-company/29589926))
- **International push:** Set up Yash Highvoltage USA Inc (2025); agency/distribution agreement with Weidmann Electrical Technology AG for parts of Europe/North Africa. ([tradebrains via search](https://tradebrains.in/yash-highvoltages-post-listing-surge-signals-a-high-voltage-growth-story/))
- **Planned fundraise:** Board considered raising up to ₹150 cr (for growth + debt repayment) around the 13 May 2026 meeting. Structure (QIP/preferential) and final approval not fully confirmed in public sources — treat as in-progress. ([search results](https://groww.in/stocks/yash-highvoltage-ltd))

---

## 2. Industry tailwinds & headwinds

**Tailwinds (well-corroborated across independent sources):**
- India T&D capex pipeline of ~₹9 trillion (~US$96.7 bn) through 2032; transmission capacity planned to expand >50% by 2030. ([IBEF](https://www.ibef.org/news/india-s-power-transmission-sector-to-see-us-96-70-billion-capex-push-by-2032))
- India transformer market ~US$3–3.8 bn (2025/26), growing ~8–8.7% CAGR to ~US$4.8–8 bn by early 2030s. ([Mordor Intelligence](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/india-transformer-market), [IMARC](https://www.imarcgroup.com/india-power-transformer-market))
- Peak demand rose 130 GW (2014) → 243 GW (2024), projected >400 GW by 2030 — structural grid build-out. ([Mordor](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/india-transformer-market))
- Bushing-specific demand drivers cited by management and analysts: renewable grid integration, data centres, EV/railway electrification, ageing-infrastructure replacement, and a global shift toward dry-type RIP bushings. ([T&D India interview](https://www.tndindia.com/we-see-strong-demand-for-rip-transformer-bushings-yash-highvoltage/))
- High barriers to entry: long utility approval/certification cycles (~6–8 years to build repeat trust), only ~13 global bushing makers — supports pricing power and stickiness. ([anigup](https://www.anigup.com/post/yash-highvoltage), [deepdivecaps](https://deepdivecaps.substack.com/p/powering-grid-reliability-through))

**Headwinds:**
- Demand is tied to the power capex cycle; tender/infrastructure delays directly hit a small order-led supplier.
- Import dependency for RIP cores and insulators — supply-chain and FX exposure until in-house production matures (management's stated goal). ([T&D India](https://www.tndindia.com/we-see-strong-demand-for-rip-transformer-bushings-yash-highvoltage/))
- Emerging domestic competition: at least one reader/analyst flags Transformers & Rectifiers (India) reportedly setting up its own bushing plant — a potential captive-supply threat. ([deepdivecaps](https://deepdivecaps.substack.com/p/powering-grid-reliability-through))

---

## 3. Company-specific risks

- **Customer concentration:** Top 10 customers ≈ 80% of revenue; heavy reliance on a few large utilities/OEMs (NTPC, POWERGRID). Partly mitigated by high switching costs/approvals. ([deepdivecaps](https://deepdivecaps.substack.com/p/powering-grid-reliability-through))
- **Working-capital intensity / cash conversion — the most concrete red flag:** Independent analysis flags CFO/PAT falling to ~0.43 in FY25 from ~1.17 in FY24, i.e., profits not converting to cash. Inventory days ~194 and cash-conversion cycle ~182 days (FY26) confirm a capital-heavy model where receivables/inventory growth can outrun profit. Debtor days (~47) look reasonable, but inventory is the pressure point. ([anigup](https://www.anigup.com/post/yash-highvoltage), [Screener](https://www.screener.in/company/544310/))
- **Export receivables / new-market execution:** US and Europe/N. Africa expansion is early-stage; export credit terms and receivable quality are not transparently disclosed. ([anigup](https://www.anigup.com/post/yash-highvoltage))
- **Execution risk on the 550 kV plant:** Commissioning/commercialization timelines (commercial output only H2 FY27) are unproven; cost overruns or delays would dent the growth thesis.
- **Raw material / import & FX:** RIP cores and insulators imported (incl. from China) until localized.
- **Liquidity / SME-stock risk:** BSE SME listing means thinner liquidity, wider spreads, and lower disclosure granularity than mainboard names. Valuation is rich — ~P/E 54, ~11x book (as of mid-June 2026), leaving little margin for execution slippage. ([Screener](https://www.screener.in/company/544310/))

---

## 4. Red flags & governance concerns

- **Cyber/payment fraud (₹2.10 cr), disclosed 31 Mar 2026:** Fraudsters impersonated a Chinese supplier via email and redirected payments (₹1.36 cr for goods received + ₹73.9 lakh paid-but-undelivered). Company called it immaterial and says it has tightened controls and filed complaints. Financially small, but it points to **weak vendor-payment verification controls** — a governance/internal-controls signal worth monitoring, especially given import dependence. ([scanx](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/companies/yash-highvoltage-limited-reports-rs-2-10-crore-cyber-fraud-incident-under-sebi-regulations/36546063))
- **Disclosure gaps:** Independent analysts note limited transparency on receivables ageing, export credit terms, the approvals/order pipeline, and related-party dealings — typical of SME-stage reporting but relevant given the cash-conversion concern. ([anigup](https://www.anigup.com/post/yash-highvoltage))
- **Promoter holding ~58%** (Mar 2026); DII ~11%, FII ~0.05%, public ~31%. No pledging surfaced in the sources I reviewed (not explicitly confirmed). ([Screener](https://www.screener.in/company/544310/))
- **No outright fraud/SEBI-action/auditor-qualification** found against the company itself; FY26 auditor opinion reported as unmodified.

---

## What I could not fully verify / confidence notes

- I relied on aggregators (Screener, scanx, anigup, tradebrains, T&D India) rather than the audited annual report or BSE filings directly — exact financial line items, the fundraise's final structure, pledging status, and segment/customer disclosures should be confirmed against the **company's filings** (yashhv.com investor portal and BSE announcements).
- The TradeBrains source returned a 403 and could not be read in full; its details were captured only via search snippets.
- The CFO/PAT and competitive-threat (Transformers & Rectifiers) claims come from single analyst write-ups — directionally consistent with the high inventory days in Screener, but treat the specific ratio and competitor plan as not independently corroborated.
- Some pages mislabel the listing venue (BSE SME, not NSE) and mix up FY25/FY26 labeling.

**This is a findings report only — no buy/sell view is implied.**

aiTitle: Equity research on Yash High Voltage

content.text:
Three research agents came back. Here's the synthesized equity research view on **Yash Highvoltage Limited**.

---

# Yash Highvoltage Ltd — Equity Research Brief

*Informational research only, not personalized investment advice. Figures are INR; FY = year ending 31 March. Data points as of ~mid-June 2026.*

## Snapshot
- **Ticker:** YASHHV / BSE code 544310 (SME platform; listed 19 Dec 2024). *Note: sources disagree on NSE Emerge vs BSE SME — verify the exact venue before trading; it's an SME listing either way, which matters for liquidity.*
- **Business:** Vadodara (Gujarat) maker of **high-voltage transformer bushings** — OIP and the higher-value RIP/RIS (dry/resin) condenser bushings, plus high-current/wall bushings and repair/retrofit services.
- **Market cap:** ~₹2,100 Cr · **P/E ~54x · P/B ~11x** · Promoter holding ~58%.

## The business in one paragraph
A bushing is ~1–2% of a transformer's cost but a single point of catastrophic failure — a niche where only ~13 makers exist globally and utility approvals take ~6–8 years, creating real barriers to entry and switching costs. Yash's edge is being an early Indian localizer of **dry-type RIP/RIS** technology (via a tie-up with Switzerland's MGC Moser-Glaser), riding "Make in India" import substitution. Customers include POWERGRID, NTPC, and OEMs like Voltamp, TARIL and Hitachi Energy India.

## Financials — strong, accelerating
| | FY22 | FY23 | FY24 | FY25 | FY26 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Revenue (₹Cr) | 65 | 90 | 108 | 150 | **235** |
| Net profit (₹Cr) | 9 | 11 | 12 | 21 | **37** |
| OPM | ~22% | ~21% | ~20% | ~21% | **~24%** |

~6x revenue in five years; FY26 sales +57%, profit +76% YoY. ROCE ~29%, ROE ~23%, debt-to-equity ~0.2. Order book **~₹400 Cr** (12–24 months visibility). A new plant extending capability to **550 kV** targets commercial production in H2 FY27.

*(Ignore the headline EPS "drop" from ₹40 to ₹13 — that's a post-IPO share-count + face-value split artifact, not an earnings decline.)*

## The bull case
- Structural tailwind: India's ~₹9 trillion T&D capex pipeline to 2032, transformer market growing ~8% CAGR, plus renewables/data-centre/EV grid demand.
- High-barrier niche with pricing power; product mix shifting to higher-margin RIP.
- Capacity expansion + US/Europe distribution (Weidmann tie-up) + Sukrut JV extend the runway.

## The risks (this is where to focus)
- **Rich valuation:** ~54x earnings, ~11x book leaves no margin for execution slippage on an SME stock.
- **Cash conversion red flag:** CFO/PAT reportedly fell to ~0.43 (FY25); inventory ~194 days, cash-conversion cycle ~182 days. Profits aren't fully converting to cash — the single most concrete fundamental concern.
- **Customer concentration:** top 10 ≈ 80% of revenue.
- **Governance signal:** a ₹2.10 Cr cyber/payment fraud (Mar 2026) — financially immaterial but points to weak vendor-payment controls. Also limited SME-level disclosure; ongoing legal proceedings noted in the prospectus.
- **Other:** import dependence (RIP cores, FX), execution risk on the 550 kV plant, a potential captive-supply threat if TARIL builds its own bushing line, and thin SME-stock liquidity. A ~₹150 Cr fundraise was also under consideration (possible dilution).

## Overall opinion
**A genuinely high-quality niche business in a strong structural uptrend — but priced for near-perfection and carrying real working-capital and governance watch-items.** The moat (approvals, RIP localization, barriers to entry) and the growth/margins are the attraction; the ~54x P/E on an illiquid SME name with weakening cash conversion is the catch. For an equity research conclusion:

- **Quality of business:** High
- **Growth:** High
- **Valuation:** Demanding / priced-in
- **Stance:** Constructive on the franchise, cautious on entry price — better suited to accumulating on pullbacks or after confirming cash-flow normalizes and the 550 kV plant ramps, than chasing at current multiples.

## Before acting, verify against primary filings
Most figures came from aggregators (Screener, Groww, ValueResearch, scanx). For a load-bearing decision, confirm against the **DRHP/RHP and BSE announcements**: (1) exact listing venue, (2) FY26 audited cash-flow statement and inventory/receivables ageing, (3) customer list, (4) the proposed ₹150 Cr raise structure, and (5) promoter pledging.

Want me to have Pax pull any of these primary filings, or go deeper on a specific angle (valuation vs. listed peers, the RIP technology moat, or the cash-flow trend)?

Deep verification task on "Yash Highvoltage Limited" (BSE SME, code 544310; CIN U40109GJ2002PLC040833). I need facts confirmed against PRIMARY sources, not aggregators where possible.

Pull and read: the company's DRHP/RHP (filed ~Oct 2024), BSE corporate announcements, the FY26 (year ended March 2026) annual results filing, and the investor section of yashhv.com. Try Chittorgarh, BSE India announcement pages, SEBI, and the company site directly.

Confirm or correct each of these specific points, citing the source for each:
1. Exact listing venue — NSE Emerge or BSE SME? (sources conflict)
2. Current promoter shareholding (latest quarter) and any PLEDGING of promoter shares.
3. The proposed ~₹150 crore fundraise — is it approved? QIP / preferential / rights? Status and intended use.
4. FY26 audited cash-flow statement: cash from operations, and how it compares to net profit (CFO/PAT).

Cross-check and report a clear, well-cited synthesis. Flag clearly anything you could NOT verify from primary sources. Report findings only, no investment view.

Deep financial-quality analysis on "Yash Highvoltage Limited" (BSE SME 544310), an Indian high-voltage transformer-bushing maker. FY = year ending 31 March.

I want a forensic look at EARNINGS QUALITY and WORKING CAPITAL, because aggregators flag that cash flow from operations is lagging reported profit. Pull data from Screener.in, Tickertape, Trendlyne, and any analyst write-ups.

Investigate and report, with figures and sources:
1. Year-by-year cash flow from operations (CFO) vs net profit (PAT) for FY22–FY26. Compute CFO/PAT ratio each year.
2. Working capital cycle trend: inventory days, debtor/receivable days, payable days, and overall cash conversion cycle, FY22–FY26.
3. Is the deteriorating cash conversion a function of rapid growth (financing receivables/inventory to scale) or a deeper quality problem? Reason it through.
4. Capex trend and how the expansion (550 kV plant, ~₹59 Cr FY26 capex) is being funded — internal accruals, debt, or the IPO/fundraise.
5. Return ratios (ROCE, ROE) trend and whether they're sustainable as the equity base grows post-IPO.
6. Any signs of aggressive revenue recognition, related-party transactions, or inventory build-up that doesn't match demand.

Give a clear, well-cited synthesis with a candid read on earnings quality. Findings only, no buy/sell call. Cite sources and figure dates.

Deep valuation analysis on "Yash Highvoltage Limited" (BSE SME 544310), an Indian high-voltage transformer-bushing maker, market cap ~₹2,100 Cr, trading ~P/E 54x, ~P/B 11x as of mid-June 2026.

I want a rigorous relative- and growth-adjusted valuation read. Pull data from Screener, Tickertape, Trendlyne, Groww.

Investigate and report, with figures and sources:
1. Build a peer comparison table: Yash vs relevant listed electrical/T&D-equipment names (e.g., Hitachi Energy India, CG Power, Transformers & Rectifiers India, GE T&D / GE Vernova T&D India, Voltamp Transformers, HBL Power, Apar Industries, Shilchar Technologies). Columns: market cap, P/E, P/B, EV/EBITDA, revenue growth, ROCE, OPM. Note which are closest comparables.
2. Where does Yash's ~54x P/E sit relative to peers and to its own growth rate (PEG)? Is the premium/discount justified by growth and margins?
3. What growth is implied by the current multiple — i.e., is it pricing in continued ~50%+ growth?
4. Historical multiple range since listing (Dec 2024) — has the stock de-rated or re-rated?
5. Brokerage/analyst price targets or fair-value estimates if any exist.
6. Key valuation sensitivities: what happens to the multiple if growth normalizes to ~20-25%?

Give a clear, well-cited synthesis on whether the valuation is demanding, fair, or cheap on a relative and growth-adjusted basis. Findings only, no explicit buy/sell. Cite sources and dates.

input.prompt:
Deep competitive-moat and industry analysis on "Yash Highvoltage Limited," an Indian maker of high-voltage transformer bushings (OIP and RIP/RIS condenser bushings), Vadodara, Gujarat.

I want to stress-test the MOAT thesis and competitive dynamics. Pull from trade press (T&D India), substack deep-dives (deepdivecaps, anigup), company materials, and industry reports.

Investigate and report, with sources:
1. How durable is the moat? Specifically test: utility approval/certification cycles (~6-8 years claimed), the ~13-global-bushing-makers claim, switching costs, and the MGC Moser-Glaser technology tie-up (what exactly does Yash get — license, cores, know-how? is it exclusive? what's the dependency risk?).
2. Competitive threats: Is Transformers & Rectifiers India (TARIL) really building captive bushing capacity? What about global players (ABB/Hitachi, Trench, Pfisterer, Siemens) entering/expanding in India? Chinese imports?
3. RIP vs OIP technology shift — is Yash genuinely ahead in Indian RIP localization, or is this catching up to where global peers already are? How big is the 550 kV capability step-up competitively?
4. Yash's actual or estimated market share in Indian bushings, and the total addressable market size for transformer bushings (India and export).
5. Import-substitution / Make-in-India angle: how much policy tailwind genuinely benefits Yash specifically vs the whole sector?
6. Customer relationships: how sticky are POWERGRID/NTPC/OEM approvals, and what's the risk of in-housing or vendor diversification?

Give a clear, well-cited synthesis with a candid verdict on moat durability. Findings only, no buy/sell. Cite sources.

Deep forward-looking analysis on "Yash Highvoltage Limited" (BSE SME 544310), Indian high-voltage transformer-bushing maker. Today is June 2026.

I want the forward outlook, catalysts, and management guidance. Pull from recent results commentary, scanx, T&D India management interviews, Groww, concall summaries if available.

Investigate and report, with sources and dates:
1. Management's stated guidance/targets for FY27 and beyond — revenue, margins, capacity, order book.
2. The ~₹400 Cr order book — composition, execution timeline, and visibility it provides.
3. The 550 kV greenfield plant — capacity addition, revenue potential, commissioning/commercial-production timeline (Q1/Q2 FY27 trial, H2 FY27 commercial?), and risks to the schedule.
4. International expansion: Yash Highvoltage USA Inc, the Weidmann Europe/N.Africa distribution deal, and export revenue ambitions — how material could exports become?
5. The Sukrut Electric 50% JV with Quality Power — strategic rationale and contribution.
6. Near-term catalysts (next 6-12 months) and the key things that could go wrong (de-rating triggers).
7. Any consensus/analyst forward estimates for FY27/FY28 earnings.

Give a clear, well-cited synthesis of the forward setup — upside drivers and downside risks. Findings only, no explicit buy/sell. Cite sources and dates.

content.text:
I now have comprehensive, cross-checked coverage. Here is my synthesis.

---

# Yash Highvoltage Limited (BSE SME 544310) — Forward Outlook, June 2026

## Framing

This report covers the forward setup for Yash Highvoltage (YHV), an Indian condenser-graded transformer-bushing maker, as of mid-June 2026 — i.e., immediately after FY26 (year ended 31 March 2026) results. I synthesize management guidance, the order book, the 550 kV greenfield plant, international expansion, the Sukrut JV, catalysts/risks, and the (thin) analyst-estimate picture. Findings only — no buy/sell.

One framing caveat up front: this is a BSE-SME microcap (~₹2,100 Cr market cap, promoter-held 57.9%). "Guidance" here comes largely from management interviews and results commentary, **not** from formal broker models — formal sell-side consensus is essentially absent (see Section 7). Treat the management targets as aspirational management guidance, not independently modeled estimates.

---

## 1. Management guidance / targets — FY27 and beyond

**FY27 (near-term):**
- **Revenue/invoicing target: ₹360–400 Cr** (vs ₹235 Cr in FY26) — implying ~40–45% growth.
- **EBITDA margin guidance: 24–25%** (maintained roughly at FY26's 25.7%).
- **Order-booking target: ₹500+ Cr** of fresh orders during FY27 (vs the ₹400 Cr stock-of-orders at FY26-end).
- **Exports target: 20%+ of revenue within 2–3 years** (from ~7% in FY26).
([multibagg.ai](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883))

**Medium/long-term (the "5-year" framing):**
- Management has publicly framed a **~300–400% revenue increase over ~5 years** (roughly 3–4x), i.e., **~30% CAGR**. ([tradebrains](https://tradebrains.in/high-growth-alert-transformer-stock-eyes-300-400-revenue-surge-over-the-next-5-years/) — note: this article was 403-blocked to me; the figures are corroborated via the search summary, but I could not read the full attribution.)
- A stated **combined revenue potential of ₹550–700 Cr** once the new Vadodara plant reaches full capacity (~3 years post-commissioning), "without needing major additional expansion." ([tradebrains "174% returns" piece](https://tradebrains.in/transformer-stock-that-delivered-174-returns-in-last-6-months-to-add-to-your-watchlist/))

**FY26 actuals (the base):** Revenue ₹235.1 Cr (+57% YoY); EBITDA ₹60.4 Cr (25.7% margin, +75%); PAT ₹37.4 Cr (15.9% margin, +75%); EPS ₹13.08; record 7,272 units sold (vs 5,752). H2 was notably stronger — 27.4% EBITDA margin. ([constructionworld](https://www.constructionworld.in/policy-updates-and-economic-news/yash-highvoltage-posts-record-fy26-consolidated-results/91679), [multibagg.ai](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883))

> Confidence: FY26 actuals — high (multiple sources agree). FY27 guidance — medium (single detailed source, multibagg's concall summary; directionally consistent with T&D interviews). The ₹550–700 Cr "full-capacity" figure — medium-low (aggregator-sourced, not a hard management commitment with a fixed date).

---

## 2. The ~₹400 Cr order book

- **Size & trajectory:** ₹400+ Cr unexecuted order book as of 31 March 2026, **up from ₹150 Cr** a year earlier (roughly 2.7x growth). ([multibagg.ai](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883), [tndindia H1FY27 piece](https://www.tndindia.com/trial-production-at-yash-highvoltage-transformer-bushing-factory-expected-in-h1fy27/))
- **Visibility:** Management frames it as **12–24 months of execution visibility**. Against an FY27 invoicing target of ₹360–400 Cr, the book covers roughly a full year-plus of revenue.
- **Product composition of revenue (proxy for book mix):** RIP bushings 83% of value, OIP ~10%, high-current ~3%, others ~4%. Geographically FY26 was ~93% domestic / ~7% exports. Retrofit/retrofit-services run ~6–7% of revenue. ([multibagg.ai](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883))

I could **not** find a granular breakdown of the ₹400 Cr book by customer, voltage class, or domestic-vs-export within the book itself — that detail does not appear in public commentary. The book is heavily RIP-weighted, consistent with management's stated "strong demand for RIP bushings" theme.

---

## 3. The 550 kV greenfield plant (Vadodara / Suncity)

This is the central forward catalyst.

| Item | Detail | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Location | Vadodara-Suncity, Gujarat (2.5 lakh sq ft land, 1.5 lakh sq ft built-up) | [tndindia](https://www.tndindia.com/trial-production-at-yash-highvoltage-transformer-bushing-factory-expected-in-h1fy27/) |
| Investment | **₹153 Cr** (capex ₹59.2 Cr already spent on PP&E in FY26) | [multibagg](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883), [scanx](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/companies/yash-highvoltage-advances-expansion-targets-trial-production-in-q2-fy-2026-27/41325144) |
| Product | RIP/RIS bushings **up to 550 kV** (vs current design capability to 220–245 kV) | scanx, multibagg |
| Capacity add | **~6,000 units/year** of RIP/RIS (vs current ~3,000 RIP units/yr at Halol) | [tndindia](https://www.tndindia.com/trial-production-at-yash-highvoltage-transformer-bushing-factory-expected-in-h1fy27/) |
| Trial production | **End Q2 FY27 (by ~Sep 2026)** | scanx, multibagg, tndindia |
| Commercial production | **H2 FY27 (~October 2026 onward)** | multibagg, constructionworld |
| Full capacity | ~3 years post-commissioning | tradebrains |

**Revenue potential:** The plate roughly doubles RIP capacity, and management ties the plant to the ₹550–700 Cr full-capacity revenue ambition. The plant is the vehicle for both **import substitution** (India's 400/765 kV bushings are largely imported today) and **export expansion** — management notes export realizations run **30–40% higher than domestic**, so the new high-voltage capacity is margin-accretive if export uptake materializes. ([tradebrains 174%](https://tradebrains.in/transformer-stock-that-delivered-174-returns-in-last-6-months-to-add-to-your-watchlist/))

**Schedule status (as of the latest commentary):** Civil work and office building complete; plant & machinery procurement complete; installation/commissioning ongoing, targeted to finish around end of Q1 FY27. ([scanx](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/companies/yash-highvoltage-advances-expansion-targets-trial-production-in-q2-fy-2026-27/41325144))

**Risks to the schedule:**
- **Type-testing / customer qualification lag** — even after commercial production starts, 400–550 kV bushings require lengthy type tests and OEM/utility homologation; revenue ramp typically trails commissioning by quarters. This is the single biggest gap between "commercial production H2 FY27" and "revenue."
- **In-house RIP-core dependency** — management is still developing in-house RIP core production to cut import reliance; the CMD acknowledges domestic supply is "concentrated." Core supply is a real bottleneck. ([tndindia interview, 25 Dec 2025](https://www.tndindia.com/we-see-strong-demand-for-rip-transformer-bushings-yash-highvoltage/))
- **Funding** — board approved a fund-raise of up to ₹150 Cr (≈₹100–110 Cr discussed); execution and dilution terms are not yet finalized. ([multibagg](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883))

> Note a minor source inconsistency: trial production is variously described as "H1 FY27" (by Sep 2026) and "end Q2 FY27." These are consistent (Q2 = the back end of H1). Commercial production is consistently H2 FY27.

---

## 4. International expansion

- **Yash HV USA Inc.** — incorporated in FY24-25 and **operationalized in FY26**; a full-time US-based BD manager hired to build the Americas footprint. The 550 kV plant is explicitly engineered to international (Americas/Europe) specs. ([multibagg](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883), [tradebrains](https://tradebrains.in/yash-highvoltages-post-listing-surge-signals-a-high-voltage-growth-story/))
- **Weidmann (Switzerland) agency & distribution deal** — a **48-month** agency agreement **effective 1 January 2025**. Weidmann Electrical Technology AG represents Yash for **OIP bushings up to 245 kV and high-current bushings up to 25 kA** across **Germany, Spain, France, Belgium, Netherlands, Luxembourg, Portugal, Morocco, Algeria, Tunisia** (Europe + North Africa). Strategic logic: Yash gets channel access during a high-demand/long-lead-time period; Yash already has 35,000+ bushings installed across 60 countries. ([Transformer Magazine](https://transformer-magazine.com/tm-news/weidmann-and-yash-announce-strategic-partnership-in-european-and-north-african-markets/), [yashhv.com](https://www.yashhv.com/post/yash-and-weidmann-announce-strategic-partnership-in-european-and-north-african-markets))
- **UK/Ireland** — distribution via Electrolink. ([multibagg](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883))

**How material could exports become?** Exports were ~7% of FY26 revenue but have grown at a ~52.8% CAGR over 3 years ([goodreturns directors' report](https://www.goodreturns.in/company/yash-highvoltage/director-report.html)). Management targets **20%+ of revenue within 2–3 years**. Given export realizations are 30–40% higher than domestic, hitting that mix would be disproportionately margin-positive. The realism of 20% hinges almost entirely on the 550 kV plant qualifying for export markets on schedule — so Sections 3 and 4 are tightly coupled. Confidence on the 20% target: medium-low (it's a stated ambition, dependent on plant ramp and overseas qualification).

---

## 5. Sukrut Electric — 50% JV with Quality Power

- **Structure:** YHV acquired **50% of Sukrut Electric Company Pvt Ltd for ₹5.24 Cr**; the other 50% held by **Quality Power Electrical Equipments**. Binding term sheet signed **9 July 2025**; deal **completed ~9 January 2026** (acquired from a German multinational seller). ([scanx](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/orders-deals/yash-highvoltage-limited-completes-5-24-crore-acquisition-of-50-stake-in-sukrut-electric-company/29589926), [tndindia](https://www.tndindia.com/quality-power-and-yash-highvoltage-to-jointly-acquire-sukrut-electric/))
- **What Sukrut does:** Pune-based (est. 1967) maker of **transformer components**, supplying hundreds of Indian and global transformer OEMs.
- **Strategic rationale:** **Backward integration** into the transformer-component ecosystem — complementary product lines, new customer/technology access, and supply-chain security at a time when component supply is a sector bottleneck. CMD Keyur Shah lists it alongside the greenfield plant and R&D as a market-share lever. ([tndindia interview](https://www.tndindia.com/we-see-strong-demand-for-rip-transformer-bushings-yash-highvoltage/))
- **Contribution:** Sukrut FY26 revenue ~₹25–26 Cr, with a management **aspiration to ₹150–160 Cr over 4–5 years**. At 50% and ₹5.24 Cr cost, it's small near-term but a cheap optionality/strategic toehold. ([multibagg](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883))

---

## 6. Near-term catalysts (6–12 months) and de-rating triggers

**Upside catalysts:**
1. **550 kV plant milestones** — trial production by ~Q2 FY27 (Sep 2026) and commercial production H2 FY27 are the headline near-term proof-points.
2. **Order-inflow momentum** — progress toward the ₹500+ Cr FY27 booking target; any large 400/765 kV import-substitution wins.
3. **Export traction** — first material orders/realizations through the US subsidiary and Weidmann channel.
4. **Fund-raise clarity** — terms of the up-to-₹150 Cr raise (de-risks plant funding, but watch dilution).
5. **Sector tailwind** — strong global transformer demand from data centers, grid/renewables, EV and railway electrification (CMD's stated demand drivers, Dec 2025).
6. **Possible NSE migration / index inclusion** as the company scales beyond SME size (general SME-graduation catalyst; not yet confirmed).

**De-rating triggers / what could go wrong:**
1. **Plant slippage** — installation, type-testing, or qualification delays pushing commercial revenue beyond FY27; this is the highest-probability disappointment vector.
2. **Valuation/expectations risk** — the stock is up ~122% over 1 year and trades at **~54x trailing P/E** (₹735, ~₹2,102 Cr m-cap, P/B 11.4x). Much of the 5-year growth is already in the price; any execution wobble de-rates hard. ([screener](https://www.screener.in/company/544310/))
3. **RIP-core / raw-material supply** — concentrated domestic supply and import dependence for cores.
4. **Margin pressure** — if export mix or pricing disappoints, the 24–25% EBITDA guide is at risk; margins already dipped at the PAT line in H1 vs H2.
5. **Cyber incident** — a reported cyber incident in FY26 (loss expensed, investigation ongoing) — minor financially but a governance/controls flag to monitor. ([multibagg](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883))
6. **Equity dilution** from the fund-raise.
7. **SME liquidity** — thin float, low institutional ownership (FII 0.05%, DII 11%), high volatility.

---

## 7. Consensus / analyst forward estimates — FY27/FY28

**There is effectively no published sell-side consensus.** I checked Trendlyne and Investing.com (the usual consensus aggregators for Indian equities) and **neither displays FY27/FY28 revenue, EPS, or target-price estimates** for YASHHV — Trendlyne shows "share price target of [blank] for Unknown Year." Screener likewise shows no forward estimates. ([Trendlyne](https://trendlyne.com/equity/consensus-estimates/2845096/13140063/yash-highvoltage-ltd/), [Screener](https://www.screener.in/company/544310/))

This is normal for a BSE-SME microcap — formal brokerage coverage hasn't initiated. The only forward numbers in circulation are **management's own**: ~₹360–400 Cr FY27 revenue, 24–25% EBITDA margin, and the ₹550–700 Cr full-capacity / 3–4x-in-5-years ambition. Anyone modeling FY27/FY28 EPS today is extrapolating from management guidance, not from independent analyst estimates.

---

## Bottom line on the forward setup

**Upside drivers:** A doubling of RIP capacity plus a brand-new 550 kV high-voltage line (commercial H2 FY27) positioned squarely at import substitution and higher-margin exports; an order book that has 2.7x'd to ₹400 Cr giving 12–24 months visibility; a structural sector tailwind (grid/data-center/renewables transformer demand); and credible international channels (Weidmann 48-month deal, US subsidiary). Management guides ~40–45% FY27 growth at ~25% EBITDA margins and a 3–4x revenue ambition over 5 years.

**Downside risks:** The whole thesis leans on one plant ramping on schedule and qualifying for export markets — type-testing and RIP-core supply are the realistic slip points. The stock already discounts a lot (~54x P/E, +122% in a year), so execution misses de-rate sharply. Add SME-specific risks (liquidity, possible dilution from the ₹150 Cr raise, a flagged cyber incident, near-zero institutional coverage).

**Evidence quality:** FY26 actuals and the plant/JV/Weidmann structural facts are well-corroborated across multiple sources. The forward numbers (FY27 guide, ₹550–700 Cr potential, 20% export target) rest mostly on management commentary relayed through a small number of outlets (multibagg's concall summary, T&D India interviews, ScanX, TradeBrains) — directionally consistent but not independently modeled, and the headline ₹400-Cr 5-year piece on TradeBrains was inaccessible to me directly (403). No formal analyst consensus exists to cross-check against.

---

### Sources
- [multibagg.ai — YHV FY26 results / concall summary](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883)
- [ConstructionWorld — YHV Posts Record FY26 Consolidated Results](https://www.constructionworld.in/policy-updates-and-economic-news/yash-highvoltage-posts-record-fy26-consolidated-results/91679)
- [ScanX — Advances expansion, targets trial production Q2 FY27](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/companies/yash-highvoltage-advances-expansion-targets-trial-production-in-q2-fy-2026-27/41325144)
- [T&D India — Trial production expected H1FY27](https://www.tndindia.com/trial-production-at-yash-highvoltage-transformer-bushing-factory-expected-in-h1fy27/)
- [T&D India — "We see strong demand for RIP bushings" interview, 25 Dec 2025](https://www.tndindia.com/we-see-strong-demand-for-rip-transformer-bushings-yash-highvoltage/)
- [T&D India — Quality Power & Yash to jointly acquire Sukrut Electric](https://www.tndindia.com/quality-power-and-yash-highvoltage-to-jointly-acquire-sukrut-electric/)
- [ScanX — YHV completes ₹5.24 Cr 50% Sukrut acquisition](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/orders-deals/yash-highvoltage-limited-completes-5-24-crore-acquisition-of-50-stake-in-sukrut-electric-company/29589926)
- [Transformer Magazine — Weidmann & Yash strategic partnership](https://transformer-magazine.com/tm-news/weidmann-and-yash-announce-strategic-partnership-in-european-and-north-african-markets/)
- [Yash Highvoltage — Weidmann partnership announcement](https://www.yashhv.com/post/yash-and-weidmann-announce-strategic-partnership-in-european-and-north-african-markets)
- [TradeBrains — Transformer stock eyes 300–400% revenue surge over 5 years](https://tradebrains.in/high-growth-alert-transformer-stock-eyes-300-400-revenue-surge-over-the-next-5-years/) (403-blocked to me; figures via search summary only)
- [TradeBrains — 174% returns / revenue potential ₹550–700 Cr](https://tradebrains.in/transformer-stock-that-delivered-174-returns-in-last-6-months-to-add-to-your-watchlist/)
- [TradeBrains — Post-listing surge / exports CAGR & US subsidiary](https://tradebrains.in/yash-highvoltages-post-listing-surge-signals-a-high-voltage-growth-story/)
- [Goodreturns — YHV Directors' Report (exports 52.8% CAGR, USA Inc.)](https://www.goodreturns.in/company/yash-highvoltage/director-report.html)
- [Screener — YHV valuation & financials](https://www.screener.in/company/544310/)
- [Trendlyne — YHV consensus estimates (no estimates published)](https://trendlyne.com/equity/consensus-estimates/2845096/13140063/yash-highvoltage-ltd/)
